# Extração: Video Ad Drafting - Step 2 of 3

- **Fonte:** Video Ad Drafting - Step 2 of 3.docx

Step 2 of 3 - Drafting Your Script

Early script writing is fun. I’ve got you started with a template and “power statements” to help build your script out. Later we’ll play around with some creative ideas, but please note down any wacky ideas as you go. 



Aim

Did You Know... (questions nearly always command attention)

If you’re looking to…













Difficulty

But here’s the problem… or But there’s a problem isn’t there...  













Understand

Look… I/we get it. It can feel ___, because ___.













Credibility

As a ____, I have helped… Here at ___, we have helped… Or, this product has helped 1000’s of _______, … and it/we can help you too.

















Action Plan

Now, the way this works is actually pretty simple. Ready to see how? Okay, well first… second… and third…















Teach

Here’s the great thing, each step is really easy to understand and implement. In fact, let me walk you through one crucial part right now... 















Exit

If you’ve found this useful, you’re going to love what I have for you next…

This [offer] has been purposefully designed so you can [insert benefits] 

To gain access right now, here’s what you’re going to do…[add specific call to action].

















5½ Additional Power Statements



THE POWER OF YOUR BUT

Gain instant trust by using this sentence structure: 

[insert drawback of your offer] BUT [insert strongest relevant feature & benefit of your offer]

E.g. Instead of saying, “The financial plan looks very positive, but, it may take some time to implement” it’s so much better to say, “It may take some time to implement, but, the financial plan looks very positive.” 



BECAUSE

People always need a reason (even if it doesn’t make total sense!):

Use the word “because” in your script because it simply works. See what I did there?! Deep down, your viewers want you, your products and your service to be their answer so they can win the day - emotionally they are ready to commit, but you’ve got to give their brain a reasonably logical explanation.



YES SET

Once you say, “yes” three times, it’s really tough to say, “no”.

So let me ask you a few questions...

Can you see how these scripting tips are going to help you write better ads? 

Are you wondering about how good your final script is going to be?

But you might be thinking about how you’re going to cut it all down to just 90 seconds…? Well, for a small investment would you be interested in having our team work on your scripts with you?

Get people internally saying “yes” 3 times and your request will become much more likely to succeed.

IGNITE THEIR IDEAL IDENTIFY

Only the very best video script writers employ this strategy.

Yep, I’m appealing to the person you want to be.

Think about who your viewers want to be and tell them if they want to be that person, they need to act and behave like it. 

This works well at the EXIT section of your script when you’re calling people to action. For example - “The best advertisers get the best results because they are the ones that actually take action and do the necessary steps. So here’s what you’re going to do next. First, click this link….”

SO YOU CAN…

Keep everything focused on the viewer and what it all means to them.

Your video is getting in front of people when they are leaning in, not leaning back. You’ve got their attention but the benefits really do need to be clearly stated to them. Video is not something you read at your own pace. It’s fast and viewers need reminding. 

The BONUS ½ POWER STATEMENT

Keep refreshing your viewer’s attention by using ‘tee-ups’, “Here’s why this is important…”, “Here’s what you ought to know…”, “If you take one thing from this video, this is it…”, “Here’s why most people get this bit wrong…”. Their brain will thank you.
